summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is 'activation energy' in the context of collision theory?

For a collision to be effective, the combined kinetic energy of the colliding molecules must equal or exceed the activation energy barrier.

What is an 'inhibitor'?

An inhibitor is the opposite of a catalyst — it slows or stops a reaction, often by blocking the active site of a catalyst or interfering with the reaction mechanism.

What is 'temperature effect' on reaction rate?

Raising temperature increases average kinetic energy. More molecules exceed the activation energy threshold, and collisions happen more frequently — both increase rate.

A car's catalytic converter reduces harmful exhaust emissions. What is the converter doing to the reactions?

Platinum and palladium catalysts in the converter lower Ea for oxidizing CO and reducing NOₓ, converting them into less harmful CO₂ and N₂.
